text.title: Risk Register Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in register-keeping practice. Each pairs symptom + cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: `last_reviewed` older than 14 days on most rows; team plans against memory, not register.
antipattern.root-cause: No weekly review ritual; ownership not assigned.
antipattern.fix: Assign register-owner role; weekly review meeting; staleness-check.py in CI.
Validator rejects non-enum values; UI uses dropdown.

antipattern.symptom: Closed R-014 reused for new risk; audit history becomes unreadable.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused stable IDs with row numbers.
antipattern.fix: Increment monotonic counter; never reuse closed IDs.

antipattern.symptom: Owner column reads 'Engineering' or a placeholder; no one accountable.
antipattern.fix: Owner MUST be a named human; teams are not owners.
